member.name: T:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.SemanticBridgeService
member.summary: Main entry point for cross-platform semantic model operations.

remarks.para: Access Pattern: This service is accessed via ScriptHost.SemanticBridge in C# scripts. It is initialized once during application startup in the main routine and remains constant for the application lifetime.

Lifecycle: Unlike ScriptHost.Model and ScriptHost.Selected, which are set per script execution, SemanticBridge is an application-level singleton service. This allows service state (such as the currently loaded MetricView) to persist across multiple script executions.
Feature Flag: Requires the SemanticBridge feature flag to be enabled for the current license tier. Construction will fail with if the feature is not enabled.

member.name: P:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.SemanticBridgeService.MetricView
member.summary: Gets the Databricks MetricView operations and state management service.
member.remarks: This service manages a single MetricView instance at a time. Loading a new MetricView replaces the current view. The view state persists across multiple script executions.

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.SemanticBridgeService.#ctor
member.summary: Create a new instance of the SemanticBridgeService. Only intended to be called in the `TryCreate` method with feature flag checks and wrapping in a `ServiceInitializationResult`.

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.SemanticBridgeService.TryCreate(TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ISemanticBridgeFeatureCheck)
member.summary: Attempts to create a SemanticBridgeService instance with structured error handling.

remarks.para: This factory method uses the Result pattern to provide explicit handling of different failure modes: - : Service initialized successfully - : Feature not enabled for license - : Unexpected initialization error
The Result includes the service instance when successful, making it self-contained and easy to use.

member.param: Feature check implementation to determine if SemanticBridge is enabled
member.returns: A containing either the initialized service instance or detailed information about why initialization failed.

member.name: T:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ServiceInitializationResult
member.summary: Represents the result of attempting to initialize the SemanticBridge service. This discriminated union provides structured error handling that distinguishes between license restrictions, unexpected failures, and successful initialization.
member.remarks: The Result pattern encapsulates both the success/failure state and the service instance, allowing callers to handle different failure modes explicitly while maintaining access to the initialized service when successful.

member.name: P:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ServiceInitializationResult.Message
member.summary: Helpful information for the user about the success or failure of the service initialization, with instructions or details in the case of failure.

member.name: T:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ServiceInitializationResult.Success
member.summary: Service was successfully initialized and is ready for use. The service instance is available via pattern matching (see example)
member.param: The successfully initialized SemanticBridgeService instance

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ServiceInitializationResult.Success.#ctor(TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.SemanticBridgeService)
member.summary: Service was successfully initialized and is ready for use. The service instance is available via pattern matching (see example)
member.param: The successfully initialized SemanticBridgeService instance

member.name: P:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ServiceInitializationResult.Success.Service
member.summary: The successfully initialized SemanticBridgeService instance

member.name: T:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.ServiceInitializationResult.LicenseFailure
member.summary: Service initialization failed due to licensing restrictions. The SemanticBridge feature is not enabled for the current license tier and the user should reach out to support to acquire an appropriate license.

member.summary: Service initialization failed due to an unexpected error beyond licensing. This typically indicates a configuration or dependency resolution issue.
member.param: Description of the initialization failure
The underlying exception, if available

The underlying exception, if available

member.name: T:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.Util.CollectionEquality
member.summary: Extension methods for collection equality comparison that supports order-independent equality checking for collections and dictionaries in AbstractModel records.
member.remarks: These methods implement set-based equality where order does not matter, supporting proper equality semantics for records containing collections. Hash codes are generated consistently to support use as dictionary keys and with MSTest assertions.

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.Util.CollectionEquality.SequenceEqualUnordered``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Compares two sequences for equality without regard to order.
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the sequences
member.param: The first sequence to compare
The second sequence to compare
member.returns: True if both sequences are null, or both contain the same elements (each element in one sequence has a corresponding equal element in the other). False otherwise.
member.remarks: This method treats sequences as sets - order does not matter, but duplicate elements are significant. Uses the default equality comparer for type T. Performance is O(n) for sequences of the same length.

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.Util.CollectionEquality.DictionaryEqual``2(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{``0,``1},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{``0,``1})
member.summary: Compares two dictionaries for equality, comparing both keys and values.
member.typeparam: The type of dictionary keys
The type of dictionary values
member.param: The first dictionary to compare
The second dictionary to compare
member.returns: True if both dictionaries are null, or both contain the same key-value pairs. False otherwise.
member.remarks: Dictionaries are considered equal if they have the same set of keys and each key maps to equal values in both dictionaries. Uses default equality comparers for both keys and values.

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.Util.CollectionEquality.GetUnorderedHashCode``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Generates a hash code for a sequence that is order-independent.
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the sequence
member.param: The sequence to generate a hash code for
member.returns: A hash code that will be the same for sequences containing the same elements regardless of order. Returns 0 for null collections.
member.remarks: The hash code is computed by combining individual element hash codes using XOR, which is commutative and associative, ensuring order independence. Duplicate elements contribute to the hash code multiple times.

member.name: M:TabularEditor.SemanticBridge.Util.CollectionEquality.GetDictionaryHashCode``2(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{``0,``1})
member.summary: Generates a hash code for a dictionary based on its key-value pairs.
member.typeparam: The type of dictionary keys
The type of dictionary values
member.param: The dictionary to generate a hash code for
member.returns: A hash code based on the dictionary's key-value pairs. Returns 0 for null dictionaries.
member.remarks: Hash codes are computed by combining individual key-value pair hash codes using XOR, ensuring that dictionaries with the same entries produce the same hash code regardless of internal ordering.
